name: API Key Management (canonical)
description: Self-service creation, listing, rotation, revocation, and per-key usage stats for platform API keys (cdb_ prefix), with per-tier key-count limits and per-tier daily/monthly request quotas. The one implementation of API-key CRUD that is auth-gated, ownership-scoped, tier-limit-correct, and has real regression coverage — reused correctly by several other flows (trial-key signup, Google/SSO login auto-provisioning, marketplace checkout) rather than reimplemented by them.

frontend.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:2129-2199 loadKeys()/createKey() call apiFetch('/api/keys'); frontend/index.html has two independent embedded widgets calling the same route (:16656-16699 and :18641-18743, cdbGenerateAPIKey/cdbRevokeAPIKey); frontend/customer-success-dashboard.html:585 is a read-only apiFetch('/api/keys') display.

navigation.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html nav includes a '🔑 API Keys' item (also visible in the live production dashboard) leading to a dedicated API Key Management section on that page.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/keyUsageBola.test.mjs:11 imports handleKeyUsage directly (tests a real BOLA/IDOR fix — workers/src/handlers/apikeys.js:133-139's ownership check). workers/test/phase4PremiumFeatures.test.mjs:18 imports handleRotateKey. workers/test/userPlanKeyLimit.test.mjs:9 and workers/test/phase8EntitlementTruth.test.mjs:20 import TIER_LIMITS from workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js. handleListKeys, handleCreateKey, and handleRevokeKey (workers/src/handlers/apikeys.js) have no direct import-based test coverage found (grep across workers/test/ for these three names returns 0 matches) — the create/list/revoke HTTP handlers themselves are the one real coverage gap in an otherwise well-tested system.

verification.evidence: workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js and workers/src/handlers/apikeys.js read in full. Router gates independently confirmed: /api/keys (GET/POST) gates on isRealUser(authCtx) at workers/src/index.js:2064 before dispatch; the three /api/keys/:id/* sub-routes (usage, rotate, DELETE) have no router-level isRealUser call but each handler independently enforces `if (!authCtx.user_id) return 401` plus ownership-scoped queries (workers/src/handlers/apikeys.js:21,76,116,129,138) — functionally equivalent protection via a different, less consistent mechanism, not a gap. Confirmed handleCreateKey enforces MAX_KEYS_BY_TIER (workers/src/handlers/apikeys.js:17) matching the pricing page's advertised per-tier key counts (comment at :12-16 documents a prior MSSP-tier miss, already fixed). Confirmed three independent, correctly-implemented consumers of the canonical createApiKey(db,userId,tier,label) 4-arg signature in the right order: workers/src/handlers/developerOnboardingHandler.js:180 (public trial-key signup funnel), workers/src/handlers/auth.js:96, workers/src/handlers/googleAuth.js:158, workers/src/handlers/ssoAuth.js:170, workers/src/handlers/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js:610 — none of these reimplement key creation; all correctly call the canonical function. Full backend suite green (see docs/capability-registry/PROGRAM_BOARD.md session log for this wave's run).

notes: This is the healthy baseline against which the other three entries in this domain are contrasted. A prior (uncommitted, lost) session's investigation described '4-5 parallel API-key systems' in this codebase; this wave independently re-verified that claim and found it accurate — in fact 5 independent create/list/revoke implementations exist (this one, plus CAP-DEVPORTAL-002, -003, -004 below, plus a 5th scoped to MSSP-partner-issued customer keys in workers/src/handlers/msspTenantPlatform.js against its own separate mssp_customer_api_keys table — that one is out of scope for this domain and belongs to the already-populated mssp.json domain; it has real ownership/tier gating and test coverage via workers/test/deadAdminChecksRestored.test.mjs, not flagged here as a problem). Three of the five hashing implementations (workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js, workers/src/handlers/developerPortal.js, workers/src/handlers/msspTenantPlatform.js) independently reimplement the identical SHA-256-hex hashing logic rather than sharing one function — a duplication smell, not a bug by itself, but the root cause of why the two genuinely broken systems below (CAP-DEVPORTAL-003, -004) drifted out of sync with the real api_keys schema: each system owns its own INSERT statement instead of going through the one function (workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js's createApiKey()) that is actually kept in sync with schema migrations. FIXED 2026-07-12 (Tier-3 backlog item #4, full-frontend-audit follow-up): GET /api/keys/{id}/usage (handleKeyUsage/getKeyUsageSummary, both already correct and already covered by workers/test/keyUsageBola.test.mjs) had no working caller on frontend/user-dashboard.html — renderKeys() gave every key row a single Revoke button and nothing else, so the adjacent Usage card's #key-usage-table permanently read 'Select a key to view usage' with no way to ever select one. Frontend-only fix: added a per-row 'Usage' button calling a new viewKeyUsage(id, label), rendering the endpoint's real response shape (today.by_module per-module breakdown, month.total) into the table. No backend change needed. 6 new static-parse regression tests (workers/test/userDashboardApiKeysUsageClickFix.test.mjs); test_coverage.has_tests above is unchanged since this adds frontend-only coverage, not new import-based coverage of any backend entry_point.

name: Self-Service Automation API Keys
description: A second, independent API-key create/list/revoke/rotate implementation exposed through the Automation dashboard, intended to let self-service (including ENTERPRISE-tier) customers generate keys for automation/workflow integrations.

frontend.evidence: frontend/automation-dashboard.html:489 loadKeys() → apiFetch('/api/self/keys'); :510 createKey() → apiPost('/api/self/keys',{label}); :526 revokeKey(); :533 rotateKey() calls POST /api/self/keys/{id}/rotate — now wired to handleRotateSelfKey (workers/src/handlers/enterpriseAutomation.js), 2026-07-09 fix.

navigation.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-12: frontend/user-dashboard.html's "Developer" sidebar section now has a real nav-item navigating to the automation dashboard page, next to the canonical API Keys link, following the same dedicated-page-navigation pattern already established for frontend/threat-intel-workbench.html. Before this fix, the only reference to "automation-dashboard" anywhere under frontend/*.html was frontend/sitemap.html — a paying customer had no discoverable path to this page at all, confirmed by a comprehensive grep across every frontend file.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/devPortalApiKeyFixes.test.mjs (2026-07-09) — 5 tests against a real in-memory D1 (node:sqlite) matching the live api_keys schema: correct-tier assertion (not orgId), per-tier key-limit enforcement (409 over quota), list returns count/max_keys, rotate succeeds and revokes the old key, anonymous caller rejected 401. workers/test/automationDashboardDiscoverability.test.mjs (2026-07-12, 4 tests) — proves the new nav-item exists, sits in a real sidebar section, follows the established pattern, and doesn't disturb the existing API Keys tab.

verification.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09 (own commit, own regression tests, following the same treatment as CAP-MASOC-001). workers/src/handlers/enterpriseAutomation.js: handleCreateSelfKey now calls createApiKey(D, userId(authCtx), userTier(authCtx), label) — correct 4-arg order — and enforces a per-tier key limit (maxSelfKeysForTier, sourced from the same TIER_LIMITS[...].api_keys the canonical POST /api/keys route enforces, so both create-key surfaces agree). handleListSelfKeys drops the broken single-arg getKeyUsageSummary(D, userId(authCtx)) call entirely (that function requires a specific keyId — a per-user aggregate isn't what it computes; nothing in the frontend consumed the resulting usage_summary field, confirmed by grep) and now returns count/max_keys (previously undefined — frontend/automation-dashboard.html:492 rendered "undefined of undefined allowed"). New handleRotateSelfKey mirrors the canonical handleRotateKey's atomic revoke-then-recreate pattern with ownership verification, wired into handleAutoRoute for POST /api/self/keys/:id/rotate (previously unmatched by any dispatch case — confirmed 404 on every click). Response shape corrected to include `key: result.raw_key` (the canonical field name) — the pre-fix response returned the createApiKey() result object verbatim, which frontend's `d.key` read as undefined even before the parameter bug is considered. FIXED 2026-07-12: both open "unknown" fields resolved by direct verification rather than left unresolved — `navigation.discoverable` (see navigation.evidence above) and `subscription_gated`: read workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js's TIER_LIMITS directly — every tier including FREE has an explicit, non-Infinity api_keys cap (FREE:1, STARTER:2, PRO:5, ENTERPRISE:20, MSSP:unlimited), so this capability is correctly available to all authenticated tiers with tier-scaled limits, exactly mirroring its canonical sibling CAP-DEVPORTAL-001 (also `subscription_gated: false`) rather than being a distinct paid-only feature — not a gap, a confirmed-correct design matching the registry description's own "including ENTERPRISE-tier" phrasing (inclusive, not exclusive).

notes: Business impact of the fix: self-service key creation via this route now grants the caller's real account tier, matches the canonical route's per-tier key-count limit, and the rotate button (previously a guaranteed 404) works. Response-shape and count/max_keys display bugs were found independently while implementing the documented fix (not present in the original CAP-DEVPORTAL-002 finding) and fixed in the same change since they were required to make the create-key flow actually functional end-to-end, not just internally correct. 2026-07-12 (22-paid-feature program, item 3): both remaining "unknown" registry fields resolved — see verification.evidence. The one real, actionable gap found this pass was navigation (page was completely undiscoverable outside the sitemap); fixed with a real nav-item. This also improves discoverability for the whole automation-dashboard.html suite (P7.0-002..009: webhooks, scheduled reports, team management, usage dashboard, governance, metrics), though those individual features were not independently re-audited in this pass — out of scope for this specific capability entry. CORRECTED 2026-07-12 (Enterprise Production Certification Program): independently re-verified — handleCreateSelfKey correctly uses userTier(authCtx) and enforces maxSelfKeysForTier, handleRotateSelfKey is wired, and the nav link is confirmed present. No issue found; priority corrected off P1 to match reality (no explicit freeze convention was recorded on this entry).

name: Developer Portal — API Explorer, SDK Generation, OpenAPI/Postman Docs, and Key Self-Serve
description: The platform's self-documenting developer portal: a machine-generated API catalog (API_CATALOG), OpenAPI 3.1 spec, Postman collection, multi-language SDK download/generation, a request playground, and its own independent API-key create/list/revoke/rotate endpoints — documented as the very first step ('Get your API key') in this portal's own published quickstart guide.

frontend.evidence: frontend/automation-dashboard.html:890-1005 calls this portal's documentation/SDK/OpenAPI/Postman endpoints (sdk/download, quickstart, the OpenAPI spec route, the Postman collection route, auth-guide, migration-guide, version-policy, examples) — those endpoints were not individually re-verified against the live schema this pass and are presumed functional pending a dedicated audit. No frontend page anywhere in frontend/*.html calls /api/developer/keys, /api/developer/keys/{id}, or /api/developer/keys/{id}/rotate (confirmed by grep) — reachable only by a developer calling the documented API directly, exactly as this portal's own quickstart instructs them to; now functional (2026-07-09 fix).

navigation.evidence: No dedicated frontend page for this portal exists; reachable only via direct API calls per its own self-published OpenAPI spec and quickstart guide (workers/src/handlers/developerPortal.js:1102), not via any customer-facing UI navigation path.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/devPortalApiKeyFixes.test.mjs (2026-07-09) — 4 tests against a real in-memory D1 (node:sqlite): unauthenticated POST rejected 401, authenticated POST succeeds and returns a real cdb_ key, cross-tenant DELETE returns 404 (no existence leak) while the owner's own list reflects the created key, and the quickstart/SDK-generator response text no longer contains the literal placeholder domain.

verification.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09 (own commit, own regression tests). Per this entry's own prior recommendation, the four broken local reimplementations (createAPIKey, hashAPIKey, listAPIKeys, revokeAPIKey, rotateAPIKey — all issuing INSERT/SELECT/UPDATE statements against columns that never existed in the live schema) were deleted entirely rather than patched, and the four /api/developer/keys* routes now delegate directly to the canonical, tested handlers in workers/src/handlers/apikeys.js (handleCreateKey/handleListKeys/handleRevokeKey/handleRotateKey — the same functions POST /api/keys uses). workers/src/index.js's dispatch now passes authCtx through (`handleDeveloperPortal(request, env, authCtx)`, previously `(request, env)` with no auth context at all), and each of the four key routes gates on isRealUser(authCtx) before dispatch, returning 401 otherwise — closing the previously-confirmed no-auth gap. Also fixed in the same pass: 18 occurrences across this file (SDK generators for Python/JS/TS/Go/curl, the OpenAPI spec's declared `servers[0].url`, quickstart, auth-guide, integration examples) of a literal unfilled template placeholder `https://your-worker.workers.dev` as the default/example base URL — every generated SDK a developer downloaded, and the OpenAPI spec itself (importable directly into Postman/Swagger), carried a non-functional domain. Corrected to `https://cyberdudebivash.in`. The API_CATALOG's documented response_schema for the four /keys endpoints was also corrected to match the canonical handlers' real response shape (it previously documented an `id` field the implementation never returned, an artifact of the old, deleted implementation).

notes: Was the highest-severity finding in this domain: a customer following this platform's own published, machine-readable quickstart guide had their very first API call fail with a 500, every time, with no auth even attempted first. Now the developer-portal key surface is not a second key system at all — it is the canonical one, reachable under a second, branded URL, so it inherits every future correctness/security improvement made to workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js for free instead of drifting again. The placeholder-baseUrl bug across SDK generators was found independently while fixing the documented finding (not part of the original CAP-DEVPORTAL-003 evidence) and fixed in the same change since a developer's first generated SDK is exactly the surface this capability exists to serve. RE-CHARACTERIZED 2026-07-12 (Enterprise Production Certification Program): this was never actually a broken journey (P1) — re-priortized to P2 (backend exists, frontend missing/partial by design). Independently re-verified the 500 fix is in place (isRealUser gating confirmed on all 4 key routes). Zero frontend pages call /api/developer/keys* today — this is an API-first developer portal, reachable via its own OpenAPI/quickstart docs, not a clickable UI panel. Recommended follow-up (not built this pass — a customer-facing key-management panel is new-feature work, out of scope for an audit/fix-only certification pass): a small UI reusing the automation-dashboard.html self-key pattern. Effort: small-medium; risk: low (backend already correct and tested).

name: Growth/Plan API Key Provisioning
description: A fourth, independent API-key provisioning path tied to the marketing/growth funnel: given an email address and a pricing plan, mints an `sap_`-prefixed key intended to gate access by plan (starter/pro/enterprise) for programmatic growth/API-economy customers, separate from both the canonical self-service flow and the trial-key signup funnel.

routes_sampled.evidence: workers/src/index.js:4169 (handleUpgradeLead — not previously catalogued as an entry point of this capability; found 2026-07-12 to also auto-provision an API key via the identical mechanism, see notes)

frontend.evidence: No frontend page found calling /api/growth/api-key or /api/growth/api-usage (grep across frontend/*.html returns 0 matches) — reachable only via direct API call, e.g. from a growth/marketing automation tool outside this repo.

navigation.evidence: No frontend surface exists for this endpoint; not discoverable via any UI navigation path.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/apiKeyHashing.test.mjs updated 2026-07-09 (mock DB regex/field names corrected from the now-fixed `plan`-column bug to `tier`, so the mock can no longer mask a real column-mismatch). workers/test/devPortalApiKeyFixes.test.mjs (2026-07-09) adds 3 tests against a real in-memory D1 (node:sqlite): provisioning is rejected 403 for an email with no verified paid lead even when the caller supplies a plan (closes the identity-check gap), provisioning succeeds and round-trips through resolveApiKey for a real server-recorded lead, and re-provisioning the same email updates the existing row rather than throwing on the invalid ON CONFLICT(email). workers/test/anonymousExposureAudit.test.mjs extended 2026-07-12 (+3 tests): POST /api/growth/upgrade rejects an anonymous caller and admits the ADMIN_KEY bypass (via the shared route-array loop), plus a dedicated pair proving the actual `UPDATE leads SET plan` write never executes for an unauthorized caller and executes exactly once for an authorized one.

verification.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09 (own commit, own regression tests). provisionApiKey (workers/src/services/apiRevenueEngine.js) now supplies every NOT NULL column (key_hash, key_prefix, via the canonical getKeyPrefix()) and uses `tier` not `plan`; the invalid ON CONFLICT(email) — SQLite rejects it outright, no UNIQUE constraint exists on that column — is replaced with an explicit SELECT-then-UPDATE-or-INSERT, avoiding any schema migration. resolveApiKey's D1 fallback query is corrected from `SELECT email, plan` (plan is not a real column — this was previously dead code, since nothing in the codebase actually calls this function's D1 path in production, confirmed by a full grep of workers/src/) to `SELECT email, tier`, mapped back to the module's own external `{email, plan}` contract so no caller-facing shape changed. Two more instances of the identical bug class were found independently in the same file while fixing the documented one: recordApiUsage's api_usage_log INSERT referenced a nonexistent `api_key`/`weight` column pair (corrected to the real `api_key_id`; `id` was also being manually supplied as a UUID into an INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT column — now omitted, letting SQLite assign it) — this function is presently unreachable dead code (imported into workers/src/handlers/growth.js, never called), fixed for correctness rather than live impact; getApiUsageSummary's three queries summed a `weight` column that was never persisted (always threw, caught, returned {error}) — corrected to COUNT(*), an honest unweighted call count instead of the previously-guaranteed 500. Separately, the actual identity-check gap in handleProvisionApiKey (workers/src/handlers/growth.js) is closed: the plan was previously trusted directly from client input whenever no lead record existed, letting any caller mint an arbitrary-tier (including enterprise) key for any email; it is now taken exclusively from the lead's own server-recorded plan (set only by the HMAC-signature-verified Razorpay webhook path — never client input), with no verified lead meaning no key, full stop. FOLLOW-UP FIXED 2026-07-11 (own commit, own regression tests): the residual gap below — sap_ keys minted successfully but unable to authenticate anywhere — is closed. workers/src/middleware/auth.js's resolveApiKey() gained a third branch (`key.startsWith('sap_')`) that delegates to this module's own resolveApiKey(env, apiKey) rather than duplicating the lookup, so rotation/revocation is honored automatically. A second, independent bug was found while writing the rotation regression test and fixed in the same change: provisionApiKey's rotation path (the `existing` UPDATE branch) updated the D1 row's hash but never invalidated the OLD hash's KV cache entry, so a rotated-away key would have kept authenticating via stale KV for up to its 1-year TTL once the sap_ branch above made this resolver reachable — now explicitly deleted (best-effort, wrapped in its own try/catch so a KV binding lacking `.delete()` can't break provisioning itself). Full chain proven with a real in-memory D1 (node:sqlite, workers/test/intelKeyResolution.test.mjs): mint → resolveAuth() authenticates at the correct tier → workers/src/handlers/intelMonetization.js's entitlementsFor() (a separate, already-STARTER-inclusive tier table this file's own TIERS constant does not need to match) grants the right entitlements; a rotated-away key is correctly rejected; an unprovisioned sap_-shaped key is correctly rejected; the pre-existing cdb_/KV/IP-fallback branches are unaffected (5 pre-existing tests in the same file still pass unchanged). Full suite green: 217 files / 2247 tests. CRITICAL FIXED 2026-07-12 (22-paid-feature program, item 4 of 22): investigating this capability's own frontend/navigation status surfaced a live, unauthenticated free-tier-theft vulnerability in a SIBLING route never previously catalogued as part of this capability. `POST /api/growth/upgrade` (`handleUpgradeLead`, workers/src/handlers/growth.js:427) was registered fully public (workers/src/index.js, comment literally read "mark lead as upgraded (public)") and, given only a client-supplied `{email, plan}` body with zero verification of any kind, called `upgradeLead()` (workers/src/services/funnelEngine.js:146) which writes `UPDATE leads SET plan = ? WHERE email = ?` — the EXACT SAME column `handleProvisionApiKey`'s 2026-07-09 fix relies on as its sole trust boundary ("plan is taken exclusively from the lead's own server-recorded plan, set only by the HMAC-verified Razorpay webhook — never client input"). `handleUpgradeLead` then immediately called `provisionApiKey(env, email, plan)` itself for any non-free plan. Net effect: any anonymous caller could POST `{email:"anything", plan:"enterprise"}` to this route, then POST their chosen email to `/api/growth/api-key`, and receive a real, fully-functional ENTERPRISE-tier `sap_` API key for the premium `/api/v1` threat-intel feed — completely bypassing payment. This reopened, via a sibling code path, the exact vulnerability class the 2026-07-09 fix documented as closed. Confirmed zero legitimate callers exist anywhere in the codebase (grepped frontend/*.html, workers/, docs/, scripts/ — 0 hits outside the route registration and handler itself) — this route's own real payment-triggered path (the actual Razorpay webhook → handlePaymentSuccess → provisionApiKey, workers/src/services/apiRevenueEngine.js:276-299) already auto-provisions keys correctly and did not depend on this route at all. FIX: gated the route behind the same `admin:business:read` RBAC check (via `requireCan`) already used for this file's 3 sibling anonymous-exposure-audit fixes (/api/growth/analytics, /api/growth/funnel, /api/growth/leads) rather than removing it outright, since — unlike CAP-MYTHOS-003's checkout — a legitimate administrative use case (support staff manually marking a lead upgraded, e.g. for a payment received through an out-of-band channel) plausibly exists and this matches the established remediation pattern for this exact file precisely. Proven closed at the database-mutation level, not just the HTTP-status level: workers/test/anonymousExposureAudit.test.mjs's new dedicated test asserts zero `UPDATE leads SET plan` writes occur for an anonymous caller and exactly one for an authorized one. Full suite green: 247 files / 2516 tests. Registry validator: 0 failures, 0 warnings. Not done: no live production round-trip (this capability has zero frontend and no confirmed external caller for its primary provisioning routes, so `customer_journey_complete` stays false pending an actual owner-confirmed live use, not because the code is unproven). A separate, lower-severity product gap was also found and deliberately NOT fixed here (see notes): a key provisioned via the real payment-webhook path has no delivery mechanism to the lead at all (no email, no UI) — the customer has no way to learn it exists short of independently calling this API a second time, something nothing currently tells them to do. FIXED 2026-07-15 (Customer Lifecycle Completion Program, Phase 4 — API Key Delivery): the key-delivery gap flagged above is closed, and the specific double-counting concern that previously blocked it (see prior notes paragraph) was independently re-verified as not applicable, not just assumed safe. Evidence: a repo-wide grep for every reader of `billing_events` (the table handlePaymentSuccess already wrote to) found zero — no revenue dashboard, analytics query, or report anywhere in workers/src/ reads that table; only `revenue_events` (written by triggerPostPurchase, read by every revenue dashboard: workers/src/handlers/revenueDashboard.js, workers/src/services/revenueEngine.js, workers/src/handlers/revenue.js, workers/src/index.js's KPI routes) feeds real reporting. This means growth-funnel payments processed via handlePaymentSuccess were previously invisible to every revenue dashboard in the platform, not merely at risk of double-counting — wiring triggerPostPurchase in fixes a second, previously-undocumented gap (growth-funnel revenue attribution) as a side effect, not a regression risk. Fix, in workers/src/services/apiRevenueEngine.js and workers/src/handlers/growth.js: all three call sites that provision a key (handlePaymentSuccess, workers/src/handlers/growth.js's handleUpgradeLead, workers/src/handlers/growth.js's handleProvisionApiKey) now capture provisionApiKey's real return value (previously discarded at 2 of the 3 sites) and pass it as meta.api_key into triggerPostPurchase(), the same lifecycle entry point workers/src/handlers/payments.js's core checkout flow already uses for this exact sequence — workers/src/services/emailEngine.js's templateSubscriptionDay0 already knew how to render meta.api_key, it simply never received one from any growth-funnel path. Separately, provisionApiKey() now also looks up `SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?` and links the new/rotated key's user_id when a real account exists (COALESCE-preserving on rotation, additive, zero schema change — api_keys.user_id already existed, growth-funnel inserts just never populated it) — this makes a linked customer's growth-funnel key visible on the CANONICAL, already-shipped 'API Keys' dashboard page (CAP-DEVPORTAL-001's listUserApiKeys(), WHERE user_id = ?) with no new frontend at all. frontend.status intentionally stays 'missing' for handleProvisionApiKey's own synchronous response path: re-confirmed zero callers exist anywhere in this repo (same grep as the 2026-07-12 finding, unchanged), so building speculative UI for an unconfirmed external caller was deliberately not done — email is now a durable backup for that path regardless of what, if anything, calls it directly. New tests: workers/test/growthApiKeyDelivery.test.mjs (8 tests) proves the real raw key (sap_-prefixed, matched by regex, confirmed distinct from the persisted hash) reaches triggerPostPurchase's meta for all 3 call sites, and proves the user_id linkage (present when an account exists, null when it doesn't, backfilled on rotation) against a real node:sqlite D1. workers/test/apiKeyHashing.test.mjs's hand-written mock D1 updated for the new user_id bind-argument position (pre-existing brittleness in the mock's positional indexing, not a production bug — the real SQL's column list and placeholder count were independently verified consistent before this was diagnosed as test-only). Full suite green: 301 files / 3177 tests.

notes: RESOLVED 2026-07-11 (see verification.evidence) — sap_ keys now authenticate end-to-end. Kept for the historical record: this note previously flagged that workers/src/middleware/auth.js's resolveApiKey had no recognition branch for the sap_ prefix (only a KV-cache lookup by raw key name, and a `cdb_`-specific D1 branch), and separately that provisionApiKey's KV cache write and this resolver's KV read used different key names (hashed vs raw) — both are now fixed. One claim in the original note needs its own correction, caught while tracing the actual call graph rather than trusting the prior description: workers/src/middleware/auth.js is NOT "the platform's core, request-path authentication resolver used by every authenticated route" — grepping every importer of that file shows exactly one consumer, workers/src/handlers/intelMonetization.js (the premium /api/v1 threat-intel feed). The platform-wide session/user resolver every other handler uses is the separate workers/src/auth/middleware.js (`resolveAuthV5`). This means the fix's actual blast radius was the intel-monetization API surface only, not the whole platform — still verified with the full suite regardless. Given three of four systems in this domain (CAP-DEVPORTAL-002, -003, -004) independently reimplemented key issuance rather than calling the one correct, tested, schema-accurate function (workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js's createApiKey(), see CAP-DEVPORTAL-001), CAP-DEVPORTAL-004 remains the one exception where full consolidation onto that function was not pursued — this subsystem's email+plan (not user_id+tier) data model and its own separate usage-tracking tables (api_usage_log, not api_key_usage) reflect a genuinely different, intentional design, not just drift, so the fix corrected its own schema/logic and auth wiring in place rather than delegating. 2026-07-12 (22-paid-feature program, item 4): a live, unauthenticated free-ENTERPRISE-key-minting vulnerability was found and fixed in the sibling /api/growth/upgrade route — see verification.evidence for the full account. Deliberately left unfixed (a genuine product-design question, not a bug): whether a provisioned key should be emailed to the lead. The platform already has a ready-built template for exactly this (emailEngine.js's templateSubscriptionDay0 conditionally renders a raw API-key block when meta.api_key is supplied, alongside a "get it from your dashboard" fallback when it isn't) but nothing in the growth/lead funnel (an email-only identity model with no dashboard login) currently populates that field — a lead who pays through this specific funnel has no way to ever retrieve their key except by independently guessing to call this API a second time. Not fixed here because: (a) it's ambiguous whether this endpoint is meant to be called by an external growth-automation tool that already handles delivery through its own separate channel, which this session cannot see or rule out; (b) wiring the full triggerPostPurchase() lifecycle helper (the established pattern payments.js uses) would also write revenue_events/funnel_events a second time for the same payment already logged by this module's own handlePaymentSuccess, risking double-counted revenue metrics if done without deeper reconciliation work. Flagged for an explicit product decision, not silently dropped. RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-12 (Enterprise Production Certification Program): the /api/growth/upgrade exploit fix is independently reconfirmed still in place (admin-gated via requireCan('admin:business:read'); workers/test/anonymousExposureAudit.test.mjs proves the DB write never executes for an anonymous caller). No broken journey found today. Priority left at P1 — per this file's own PROGRAM_BOARD.md-documented historical-severity convention for this exact entry ("priority stays P1, untouched, per this file's historical-severity convention") — not an oversight; this correction only adds re-verification evidence.
